{{Short description|Film award ceremony in 2020}} {{Use mdy dates|date=March 2025}} {{Infobox film awards | number = 77 | award = Golden Globe Awards | caption = Official poster | image = 77th Golden Globe Awards.png | date = January 5, 2020 | site = [[The Beverly Hilton]],
[[Beverly Hills, California]], U.S. | host = [[Ricky Gervais]] | director = [[Louis J. Horvitz]] | best_film_drama = ''[[1917 (2019 film)|1917]]'' | best_film_musical_or_comedy = ''[[Once Upon a Time in Hollywood]]'' | best_drama_series = ''[[Succession (TV series)|Succession]]'' | best_musical_or_comedy_series = ''[[Fleabag]]'' | best_miniseries_or_tv_movie = ''[[Chernobyl (miniseries)|Chernobyl]]'' | most_wins = ''Once Upon a Time in Hollywood'' (3) | most_nominations = ''[[Marriage Story]]'' (6) | network = [[NBC]] | ratings = 18.3 million ([[Nielsen Media Research#Nielsen TV ratings|Nielsen ratings]]){{cite web |last1=Thorne |first1=Will |last2=Otterson |first2=Joe |title=How Golden Globes Ratings Stack Up Against Oscars, Emmys, Grammys |url=https://variety.com/2020/tv/news/tv-ratings-golden-globes-oscars-emmys-grammys-1203458552/ |language=en-US |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=January 6, 2020 |access-date=February 1, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200108012425/https://variety.com/2020/tv/news/tv-ratings-golden-globes-oscars-emmys-grammys-1203458552/ |archive-date=January 8, 2020 |url-status=live}} | last = [[76th Golden Globe Awards|76th]] | next = [[78th Golden Globe Awards|78th]] }} The '''77th Golden Globe Awards''' honored the best in [[2019 in film|film]] and [[2019 in American television|American television of 2019]], as chosen by the [[Hollywood Foreign Press Association]]. Produced by [[Dick Clark Productions]] and the HFPA, the ceremony was broadcast live on January 5, 2020, from [[The Beverly Hilton]] in [[Beverly Hills, California]] beginning at 5:00 p.m. [[Pacific Time Zone|PST]] / 8:00 p.m. [[Eastern Time Zone|EST]]. The ceremony aired live on [[NBC]] in the United States. [[Ricky Gervais]] hosted the ceremony for the fifth and "final" time.{{cite web |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2019/11/12/movies/ricky-gervais-golden-globes.html |title=Ricky Gervais Will Return to Host (and Roast) the Golden Globes |last=Buchanan |first=Kyle |date=November 12, 2019 |work=[[The New York Times]] |access-date=November 13, 2019 |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191113014624/https://www.nytimes.com/2019/11/12/movies/ricky-gervais-golden-globes.html |archive-date=November 13, 2019 |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2020/01/05/movies/golden-globes.html |title='Once Upon a Time … in Hollywood' and '1917' Win Top Awards at Golden Globes |last1=Barnes |first1=Brooks |last2=Sperling |first2=Nicole |date=January 5, 2020 |work=[[The New York Times]] |access-date=January 6, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200106061237/https://www.nytimes.com/2020/01/05/movies/golden-globes.html |archive-date=January 6, 2020 |url-status=live}} The nominees were announced on December 9, 2019, by [[Tim Allen]], [[Dakota Fanning]] and [[Susan Kelechi Watson]].{{cite magazine |url=https://ew.com/golden-globes/2019/12/05/tim-allen-dakota-fanning-susan-kelechi-watson-golden-globe/ |title=Tim Allen, Dakota Fanning, and Susan Kelechi Watson to announce 2020 Golden Globe nominations |last=Huff |first=Lauren |date=December 5, 2019 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |access-date=December 9, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191209050051/https://ew.com/golden-globes/2019/12/05/tim-allen-dakota-fanning-susan-kelechi-watson-golden-globe/ |archive-date=December 9, 2019 |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2019/12/golden-globes-nominations-announced-updating-live-1202804135/ |title=Golden Globes Nominations: 'Marriage Story', Netflix, 'Once Upon a Time in Hollywood' Lead Way in Film – Full List of Nominations |last=Hipes |first=Patrick |date=December 9, 2019 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |access-date=December 10, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191210132813/https://deadline.com/2019/12/golden-globes-nominations-announced-updating-live-1202804135/ |archive-date=December 10, 2019 |url-status=live}} ''[[Marriage Story]]'' earned a leading six nominations. [[Tom Hanks]] and [[Ellen DeGeneres]] were announced as the recipients of the [[Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award|Cecil B. DeMille Award]] and the [[Golden Globe Carol Burnett Award|Carol Burnett Award]], respectively. ''[[Once Upon a Time in Hollywood]]'' won the most awards for the ceremony with three, including [[Golden Globe Award for Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy|Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy]]. ''[[1917 (2019 film)|1917]]'', ''[[Joker (2019 film)|Joker]]'', and ''[[Rocketman (film)|Rocketman]]'' won two awards each, with ''1917'' winning [[Golden Globe Award for Best Motion Picture – Drama|Best Motion Picture – Drama]]. For television, ''[[Chernobyl (miniseries)|Chernobyl]]'', ''[[Fleabag]]'' and ''[[Succession (TV series)|Succession]]'' were the most awarded, with two wins each.{{cite web |last=Lang |first=Brent |url=https://variety.com/2020/film/awards/golden-globes-2019-biggest-wins-netflix-1203457082/ |title=Golden Globes: 'Once Upon a Time in Hollywood', '1917' Win Big |date=January 5, 2020 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |access-date=January 6, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200108034816/https://variety.com/2020/film/awards/golden-globes-2019-biggest-wins-netflix-1203457082/ |archive-date=January 8, 2020 |url-status=live}} The ceremony was nominated for two [[Primetime Emmy Awards]] ([[Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Variety Special (Live)|Outstanding Variety Special (Live)]] and [[Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Production Design for a Variety Special|Outstanding Production Design for a Variety Special]]).{{cite web |url=https://www.televisionacademy.com/shows/77th-annual-golden-globe-awards |title=77th Annual Golden Globe Awards |publisher=[[Academy of Television Arts & Sciences]] |access-date=September 15, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250211230539/https://www.televisionacademy.com/shows/77th-annual-golden-globe-awards |archive-date=February 11, 2025 |url-status=live}} ==Winners and DeMille Award=== The [[Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award|Cecil B. DeMille Award]] is an honorary award bestowed for outstanding contributions to the world of entertainment. It is awarded to honorees who have made a significant mark in the film industry and is named after its first recipient, director [[Cecil B. DeMille]]. * [[Tom Hanks]]{{cite web |url=https://www.goldenglobes.com/cecil-b-demille-award-0 |title=Cecil B. DeMille Award |publisher=[[Hollywood Foreign Press Association]] |language=en-US |access-date=November 13, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191123205901/https://www.goldenglobes.com/cecil-b-demille-award-0 |archive-date=November 23, 2019 |url-status=live}} ===Carol Burnett Award=== The [[Golden Globe Carol Burnett Award|Carol Burnett Award]] is an honorary award given for outstanding and lasting contributions to television on or off the screen. It is named in honor of its first recipient, actress [[Carol Burnett]]. * [[Ellen DeGeneres]]{{cite web |url=https://www.goldenglobes.com/carol-burnett-award-0 |title=Carol Burnett Award |publisher=[[Hollywood Foreign Press Association]] |language=en-US |access-date=November 13, 2019 ceremony:{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/charlize-theron-will-ferrell-tiffany-haddish-presenting-golden-globes-1265542/ |title=Golden Globes: Leonardo DiCaprio, DeMille Award * [[Antonio Banderas]] and [[Helen Mirren]] with Best Director * [[Cate Blanchett]] introduced ''[[Joker (2019 film)|Joker]]'' * [[Tiffany Haddish]] and [[Salma Hayek]] with Best Actress – Miniseries or Television Film and Best Miniseries or Television Film * [[Da'Vine Joy Randolph]] and [[Wesley Snipes]] introduced ''[[Dolemite Is My Name]]'' * [[Jennifer Lopez]] and [[Paul Rudd]] with Best Original Score and Best Supporting Actor – Motion Picture * [[Chris Evans (actor)|Chris Evans]] and [[Scarlett Johansson]] with Best Actor – Motion Picture Musical or Comedy and Best Actress – Motion Picture Musical or Comedy * [[Jason Bateman]] and [[Naomi Watts]] introduced ''[[Marriage Story]]'' * [[Rachel Weisz]] introduced ''[[The Two Popes]]'' * [[Pierce Brosnan]] and [[Will Ferrell]] with Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy * [[Glenn Close]] with Best Actor – Motion Picture Drama * [[Rami Malek]] with Best Actress – Motion Picture Drama * [[Sandra Bullock]] with Best Motion Picture – Drama ==Reception to Ricky Gervais' opening monologue== {{tweet | text = 1. Simply pointing out whether someone is left or right wing isn't winning the argument.
2. If a joke is good enough, it can be enjoyed by anyone.
3. It's not all about you.
4. Just because you're offended, doesn't mean you're right. | name = Ricky Gervais | username = rickygervais | date = January 8, 2020 | ID = 1214846542210904065 }} Criticism arose following [[Ricky Gervais]]' opening monologue, which was seen as an attack on the perceived hypocrisy of [[Cinema of the United States|Hollywood]].{{cite web |last=Moniuszko |first=Sara M. |title=After his controversial Golden Globes hosting, Ricky Gervais defends his humor |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/celebrities/2020/01/08/ricky-gervais-defends-his-humor-after-hosting-2020-golden-globes/2842320001/ |work=[[USA Today]] |date=January 8, 2020 |access-date=January 9, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200108214858/https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/celebrities/2020/01/08/ricky-gervais-defends-his-humor-after-hosting-2020-golden-globes/2842320001/ |archive-date=January 8, 2020 |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-01-06/ricky-gervais-opening-at-the-golden-globes-2020/11844260 |title=From DiCaprio to Dench: Stars react to Gervais' Golden Globes tongue-lashing |last=Fuller |first=Peta |date=January 6, 2020 |publisher=[[ABC News (Australia)|ABC News]] |language=en-AU |access-date=January 6, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200106060413/https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-01-06/ricky-gervais-opening-at-the-golden-globes-2020/11844260 |archive-date=January 6, 2020 |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://www.theatlantic.com/entertainment/archive/2020/01/golden-globes-ricky-gervais-doublethink-hollywood/604486/ |title=Ricky Gervais Almost Got It Right on Hollywood Hypocrisy |last=Kornhaber |first=Spencer |date=January 6, 2020 |work=[[The Atlantic]] |language=en-US |access-date=January 6, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200106204248/https://www.theatlantic.com/entertainment/archive/2020/01/golden-globes-ricky-gervais-doublethink-hollywood/604486/ |archive-date=January 6, 2020 |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://www.ccn.com/ricky-gervais-blasted-apple-sweatshops-at-golden-globes-but-hes-not-woke-either/ |title=Ricky Gervais Blasted Apple 'Sweatshops' at Golden Globes – But He's Not Woke Either |last=Messamore |first=W. E. |date=January 6, 2020 |publisher=CCN |language=en-US |access-date=January 6, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200112065758/https://www.ccn.com/ricky-gervais-blasted-apple-sweatshops-at-golden-globes-but-hes-not-woke-either/ |archive-date=January 12, 2020 |url-status=dead}} He joked about several controversial topics, such as [[Death of Jeffrey Epstein|the death]] of [[Jeffrey Epstein]], the [[Varsity Blues scandal|college admissions scandal]] and the middle-aged [[Leonardo DiCaprio]]'s dating history with younger women. Gervais also jokingly accused the [[Hollywood Foreign Press Association|Hollywood Foreign Press]] of racism for its lack of diversity and an "In Memoriam" section, mocked [[Amazon (company)|Amazon]], [[Apple Inc.|Apple]] and [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] for their [[unfair labor practice]]s, and chastised awardees who talk about their political views in acceptance speeches. Gervais' comments attained mixed reactions across the [[political spectrum]]. While [[Conservatism in the United States|conservatives]] highly praised Gervais,{{cite web |last=Kim |first=Violet |title=Conservatives Really, Really Loved Ricky Gervais' Golden Globes Monologue |url=https://slate.com/culture/2020/01/ricky-gervais-golden-globes-monologue-conservative-reaction.html |work=[[Slate (magazine)|Slate]] |date=January 6, 2020 |access-date=January 8, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200108020829/https://slate.com/culture/2020/01/ricky-gervais-golden-globes-monologue-conservative-reaction.html |archive-date=January 8, 2020 |url-status=live}} journalists from [[Modern liberalism in the United States|liberal]] outlets were more critical,{{cite web |last=McFarland |first=Melanie |title=Why the Golden Globes and host Ricky Gervais felt particularly pointless |url=https://www.salon.com/2020/01/06/golden-globes-ricky-gervais-pointless-hfpa/ |work=[[Salon.com]] |date=January 6, 2020 |access-date=January 8, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200108074837/https://www.salon.com/2020/01/06/golden-globes-ricky-gervais-pointless-hfpa/ |archive-date=January 8, 2020 |url-status=live}}{{cite web
